alignments.preprint_claim_text: An optimization model is proposed to analyze the impact of making a change to an existing case mix. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The universal approximation theorem is generalized to uniform convergence on the noncompact input space $\text{R}^n$. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: For all $n$ and all $l\geq2$, the vector space of functions that are uniformly approximable by neural networks with $l$ hidden layers and $n$ inputs is an algebra under the pointwise product. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.rationale: The publication makes this conditional independence more explicit and adds a constructive description (closed span of products of sigmoids) and higher certainty, so the claim is clarified and its confidence amplified.

alignments.rationale: The publication states the same identification more precisely (including l≥1) and with higher certainty, so the claim is clarified and confidence amplified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The SGU-MLP classification algorithm demonstrates superiority over several CNN and CNN-ViT-based models. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The SGU-MLP classification algorithm demonstrates superiority over several CNN- and CNN-ViT-based models for precise LULC mapping. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Both statements assert SGU-MLP's superiority; the publication adds the application context (precise LULC mapping) but does not change the core claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The SGU-MLP model consistently outperforms benchmark CNN and CNN-ViT-based algorithms. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The SGU-MLP classification algorithm demonstrates superiority over several CNN- and CNN-ViT-based models for precise LULC mapping. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: This is a restatement of the same performance claim; publication frames it in the LULC mapping context but keeps the substantive result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: In the Houston experiment, SGU-MLP significantly outperformed HybridSN, CoAtNet, Efficientformer, iFormer, and ResNet by approximately 15%, 19%, 20%, 21%, and 25%, respectively, in terms of average accuracy. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The SGU-MLP classification algorithm demonstrates superiority over several CNN- and CNN-ViT-based models for precise LULC mapping. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the superiority claim but omits the detailed per-model percentage differences presented in the preprint, so the claim is clarified to a more general superiority statement.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Current advanced multilayer perceptrons (MLPs) can provide viable alternatives to both deep CNNs and ViTs when training data is limited. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: This broader conclusion about MLPs as alternatives is present in the publication but was not listed among the provided preprint claims, so it is treated as an added claim.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Vision transformers (ViTs) achieve superior modeling of global contextual information compared to convolutional neural networks (CNNs). (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication contains this general conclusion about ViTs that does not appear in the preprint claim list, so it is marked as an added claim.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The SGU-MLP effectively combines MLPs and spatial gating units (SGUs) for precise LULC mapping using multimodal data. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Although related to the SGU-MLP name, this explicit formulation of the model architecture and multimodal contribution was not listed among the provided preprint claims and is therefore treated as added.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Physics-informed machine learning (PIML) techniques improve the generalization of models and ensure compliance with governing physical laws. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Physics-informed machine learning (PIML) techniques improve the generalization of models by integrating physics principles into data-driven approaches. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Both emphasize improved generalization via physics integration; the publication omits the stronger phrasing about 'ensure compliance with governing physical laws,' so the claim is slightly clarified to a more measured statement.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: PIML enhances the interpretability of machine learning models by integrating physics principles into data-driven approaches. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: PIML enhances the interpretability of machine learning models in applications related to subsurface energy systems. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Both assert that PIML improves interpretability via physics integration; the publication situates this specifically in subsurface energy applications but retains the same substance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The review highlights successful applications of PIML in subsurface energy systems, particularly in the oil and gas industry. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The review highlights the successful utilization of PIML for seismic applications, reservoir and fluid flow simulation, and hydrocarbons production forecasting. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication provides more specific examples and phrases these as successful utilizations with higher certainty compared to the preprint's more general highlighting statement.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: PIML has the potential to revolutionize the oil and gas industry by providing more accurate and reliable predictions for resource management. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: PIML has the potential to revolutionize the oil and gas industry by providing more accurate and reliable predictions for resource management and operational efficiency. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The core conclusion about PIML's transformative potential in oil and gas is preserved, with the publication adding 'operational efficiency' but not changing the claim's substance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed model named Long Short-Term Memory with Sequential Self-Attention Mechanism (LSTM-SSAM) predicts future stock prices with minimal errors. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed model called long short-term memory with sequential self-attention mechanism (LSTM-SSAM) effectively predicts future stock prices with minimal errors. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same model claim with near-identical wording and emphasis on effective, low-error prediction; only label type (result vs contribution) differs.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The experimental results prove the effectiveness and feasibility of the proposed model compared to existing models. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Extensive experiments on three stock datasets (SBIN, HDFCBANK, and BANKBARODA) prove the effectiveness and feasibility of the LSTM-SSAM model compared to existing models. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication provides dataset detail but conveys the same conclusion about effectiveness and feasibility versus existing models.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The root-mean-squared error (RMSE) and R-square (R2) evaluation indicators show the best results for the proposed model. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The experimental findings demonstrate that the root-mean squared error (RMSE) and R squared (R2) evaluation indicators yield the best results for stock price prediction. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed Hierarchical Alignment Transformers (HAT) framework unifies the encoder architectures for both images and texts using Transformers. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.pub_claim_text: HAT produces representations with more similar characteristics for images and texts, facilitating easier interactions and alignments between them. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Experimental results demonstrate that HAT outperforms state-of-the-art baselines by a large margin on benchmark datasets. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Experimental results demonstrate that HAT outperforms state-of-the-art baselines by a large margin on benchmark datasets. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: On the MSCOCO dataset, HAT achieves a 7.6% relative score improvement of Recall@1 for image-to-text retrieval and 16.7% for text-to-image retrieval. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: On the MSCOCO dataset, HAT achieves a 7.6% relative score improvement of Recall@1 for image-to-text retrieval and 16.7% for text-to-image retrieval. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: On the Flickr30K dataset, HAT achieves a 4.4% relative score improvement of Recall@1 for image-to-text retrieval and 11.6% for text-to-image retrieval. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: On the Flickr30K dataset, HAT achieves a 4.4% relative score improvement of Recall@1 for image-to-text retrieval and 11.6% for text-to-image retrieval. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same Flickr30K quantitative improvements.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Existing image-text matching methods primarily use the most similar samples as hard negatives, which may not be true negatives. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Existing image-text matching methods primarily use the most similar samples as hard negatives, which may not be true negatives. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same diagnostic observation about hard negatives in image-text matching.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Repelling false negatives in triplet loss misleads semantic representation learning and results in inferior retrieval performance. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Repelling false negatives in triplet loss misleads semantic representation learning and results in inferior retrieval performance. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed False Negative Elimination (FNE) strategy effectively alleviates the problem introduced by false negatives. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed False Negative Elimination (FNE) strategy alleviates the problem introduced by false negatives in image-text matching. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Extensive experiments on Flickr30K and MS-COCO demonstrate the superiority of the proposed false negative elimination strategy. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Extensive experiments on Flickr30K and MS-COCO demonstrate the superiority of the proposed false negative elimination strategy. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The AI model with both salient features and counterfactual explanations improved performance and agreement levels for therapists and laypersons when 'right' AI outputs were presented. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The AI model with both salient features and counterfactual explanations improved performance and agreement levels for therapists and laypersons when 'right' AI outputs were presented. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Counterfactual explanations reduced over-reliance on 'wrong' AI outputs by 21% compared to salient feature explanations. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Laypersons experienced higher performance degrades than therapists when using both types of explanations. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Laypersons experienced higher performance degrades than therapists when using both types of explanations. (certainty=medium, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Counterfactual explanations have the potential to better estimate the accuracy of an AI model and improve human-AI collaborative decision-making. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Counterfactual explanations have the potential to better estimate the accuracy of an AI model and improve human-AI collaborative decision-making. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.pub_claim_text: A novel self-supervised learning framework is proposed for acquiring accurate and rotation-invariant 3D point set features at object-level.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Mainstream model-agnostic explanation methods, such as the Shapley values, are not suitable for probabilistic anomaly attribution due to their deviation-agnostic property.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Mainstream model-agnostic explanation methods, such as the Shapley values, are not suitable for probabilistic anomaly attribution due to their deviation-agnostic property.

alignments.pub_claim_text: We propose a novel framework for probabilistic anomaly attribution that computes attribution scores as the predictive mean and quantifies the uncertainty of those scores.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed framework considers a generative process for perturbations that counter-factually bring the observed anomalous observation back to normalcy.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed framework considers a generative process for perturbations that counter-factually bring the observed anomalous observation back to normalcy.

alignments.pub_claim_text: We introduce a variational Bayes algorithm for deriving the distributions of per variable attribution scores.

alignments.pub_claim_text: This is the first probabilistic anomaly attribution framework that is free from being deviation-agnostic.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Experimental results validate the effectiveness of PaInvRL for both within-and cross-environmental learning on three public multimedia recommendation datasets.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: All models achieved accurate predictions of speech fluency ratings, with average root-mean-square errors as low as 0.5.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The MLR yielded a correlation coefficient of 0.87 with reference ratings at the sentence level, and of 0.93 when aggregating the data for each participant.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The inclusion of an additional predictor sensitive to repetitions improved further the predictions with a correlation coefficient of 0.91 at the sentence level, and of 0.96 at the participant level.

alignments.rationale: The publication narrows the generic evaluation claim to the concrete question of XGBoost vs a deep model on the specific dataset, clarifying the study aim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Incorporating more features into the machine learning models can enhance model complexity but may also increase the presence of missing values in the dataset.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The analysis reveals a trade-off between incorporating more features to enhance model complexity and the resulting presence of increased missing values in the dataset.
alignments.rationale: The publication states the same trade-off as observed in the preprint without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The early-life features extracted from capacity-voltage data correlate strongly with cell lifetime.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The early-life features derived from capacity-voltage data can predict the lifetime of lithium-ion cells cycled under varying conditions.
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the correlation as a predictive capability and explicitly mentions varying cycling conditions, making the claim more specific/broader in applicability.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Using a newly generated dataset from 225 nickel-manganese-cobalt/graphite Li-ion cells, we demonstrate a lifetime prediction with 15.1% mean absolute percentage error using no more than the first 15% of data.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed lifetime prediction method achieves a mean absolute percentage error (MAPE) of 15.1% for in-distribution cells.
alignments.rationale: The numerical performance is preserved but the publication specifies the result is for in-distribution cells (a narrower framing) and omits the explicit "first 15% of data" detail.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Further testing with a hierarchical Bayesian regression model achieves 21.8% mean absolute percentage error for out-of-distribution cells.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A hierarchical Bayesian model improves performance on extrapolation, achieving a MAPE of 21.8% for out-of-distribution cells.
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the same performance and role for the hierarchical Bayesian model on out-of-distribution/extrapolation tasks with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our approach highlights the importance of using domain knowledge of lithium-ion battery degradation modes to inform feature engineering.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study emphasizes the importance of domain knowledge of battery degradation in feature engineering and model construction.
alignments.rationale: Both versions convey the same conclusion about the importance of domain knowledge in feature engineering; wording is essentially equivalent.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We provide the community with a new publicly available battery aging dataset with cells cycled beyond 80% of their rated capacity.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A new publicly available battery lifelong aging dataset has been provided.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the contribution of a new public dataset but uses slightly different wording; the core contribution (new dataset) is preserved.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The neural network approach effectively computes stream functions that are orthogonal to a given vector field.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The neural network approach effectively computes stream functions from a given vector field.
alignments.rationale: The publication drops explicit mention of orthogonality but preserves the core result that the network computes stream functions from the vector field, a modest revision in wording.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The network learns to map input coordinates to a stream function value by minimizing the inner product of the gradient of the neural network's output and the vector field.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The network learns to map input coordinates to a stream function value by minimizing the inner product of the gradient of the neural network’s output and the vector field.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same training objective with equivalent wording.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Optional regularizing loss functions can be used to generate stream function solutions that follow the flow field's curvature.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Optional constraints and regularizing loss functions can be used to generate specific stream function solutions that follow the flow field’s curvature.
alignments.rationale: The claim about optional regularization to follow curvature is preserved, with only small wording additions about constraints.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The approach allows for the learning of a stream function that includes a stream surface passing through a seeding rake.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The approach allows for the visualization of stream surfaces to analyze flow features.
alignments.rationale: The publication shifts focus from the specific seeding-rake construction to more general visualization of stream surfaces, broadening the claimed utility.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The results of the proposed method are compared with other implicit solutions, providing both qualitative and quantitative results for various vector fields.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The results of the neural network approach are compared with other implicit solutions, demonstrating qualitative and quantitative improvements.
alignments.rationale: The publication not only compares results but asserts improvements, amplifying the confidence/impact of the result compared to the preprint's more neutral phrasing.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: CLAIRE effectively mitigates the biases from the sensitive attribute using a representation learning framework based on counterfactual data augmentation and an invariant penalty.
alignments.pub_claim_text: CLAIRE effectively mitigates the biases from the sensitive attribute using a representation learning framework based on counterfactual data augmentation and an invariant penalty.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same methodological claim with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Experiments conducted on both synthetic and real-world datasets validate the superiority of CLAIRE in both counterfactual fairness and prediction performance.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Experiments conducted on both synthetic and real-world datasets validate the superiority of CLAIRE in both counterfactual fairness and prediction performance.
alignments.rationale: Experimental validation and claimed superiority are preserved identically in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Existing methods for achieving counterfactual fairness require prior human knowledge of the causal model for the data, which is often unknown in real-world scenarios.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Existing methods for achieving counterfactual fairness require prior human knowledge of the causal model for the data.

alignments.rationale: The publication expands the point by explicitly noting that causal models are often unknown in real-world settings, clarifying the risk described in the preprint.

alignments.pub_claim_text: NEAT mitigates confounding biases in causal effect estimation based on the treatment assignment modeling.
alignments.rationale: The claim about mitigating confounding biases is preserved verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method is extended into a dynamic setting to handle time-varying observational data.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The method is extended into a dynamic setting to handle time-varying observational data.
alignments.rationale: The contribution to dynamic/time-varying settings is unchanged in the publication.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Experiments on synthetic datasets and a real-world MRSA dataset validate the effectiveness of the proposed method.
alignments.rationale: Experimental validation claims are identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The Interactive Spatiotemporal Token Attention Network (ISTA-Net) effectively models spatial, temporal, and interactive relations in human-robot interaction.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Entity Rearrangement is proposed to eliminate the orderliness in ISTs for interchangeable entities, which is crucial for recognizing interactive actions.

alignments.pub_claim_text: PiTL-generated pairs demonstrate effectiveness in enhancing vision-language pre-training with less supervision. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same finding about individual trial-and-error prompt crafting, with only minor wording differences.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study identifies distinct pathways in the prompt engineering process used by EFL students.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Six distinct types of prompt content were identified, including sophisticated combinations such as natural language instruction with a question and an input data exemplar.
alignments.rationale: The publication specifies the nature and number of distinct types (six) and gives examples, making the preprint's more general 'distinct pathways' claim more concrete.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: There is a need to provide prompt engineering education in the EFL writing classroom.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The findings evidence the need for explicit prompt engineering education in EFL writing classrooms.
alignments.rationale: The conclusion about the need for explicit prompt-engineering instruction is preserved and restated in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Students can benefit from learning a greater variety of prompt content and more sophisticated prompts.
alignments.pub_claim_text: With personalized and collaborative instruction on prompt content, sequence, and capabilities, students can become more strategic collaborators with ChatGPT.
alignments.rationale: The publication expands the original suggestion into a more detailed instructional recommendation (personalized, collaborative, sequence, capabilities), broadening the practical scope of the preprint's pedagogical claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A deep learning model with predictive uncertainty was developed that runs on a static website without requiring installation.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A deep learning model with predictive uncertainty was developed that runs on a static website without requiring installation.
alignments.rationale: The contribution is stated identically in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model achieves satisfactory results in solubility prediction.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The model achieves satisfactory results in solubility prediction.
alignments.rationale: The result is reported the same way in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The approach removes the need to pay for and maintain servers by shifting computing needs onto the website visitor.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The approach removes the need to pay for and maintain servers by shifting computing needs onto the website visitor.
alignments.rationale: The conclusion about cost/maintenance trade-offs is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The work demonstrates how to create molecular property prediction models that balance uncertainty and ease of use.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The work demonstrates how to create molecular property prediction models that balance uncertainty and ease of use.
alignments.rationale: The concluding demonstration claim appears verbatim in the publication.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed end-to-end learnable video codec outperforms both state-of-the-art learned and conventional video codecs in its base layer. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same result verbatim with the same emphasis and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The codec maintains comparable performance on the human vision task in its enhancement layer. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The codec maintains comparable performance on the human vision task in its enhancement layer. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The claim is the same but the publication expresses higher certainty, so confidence is amplified while content is unchanged.

alignments.pub_claim_text: A new paradigm for video coding is needed to facilitate efficient representation and compression of video for both machine and human use. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Alioth achieves an average mean absolute error of 5.29% offline and 10.8% when testing on applications unseen in the training stage. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Quantum random access memory (QRAM) can accommodate up to $\text{O}(10^7)$ logical qubits in 1 dimension, $\text{O}(10^{15})$ to $\text{O}(10^{20})$ in various 2D architectures, and $\text{O}(10^{24})$ in 3 dimensions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A feasible QRAM model in hybrid quantum acoustic systems can support a significant number of logical qubits across different dimensions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication drops the explicit asymptotic numerical estimates and frames the result as feasibility in a specific hybrid acoustic model; the claim is therefore a narrowed, less numerically specific revision with somewhat reduced emphasis.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The causality bound we explore broadly applies to other quantum hardware systems. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Relativistic causality principles could universally influence quantum computing hardware. (certainty=low, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Both claims relate causality/relativistic constraints to quantum hardware, but the publication couches the claim more cautiously ('could') and at lower certainty, so the claim is weakened (revised) though scope remains broadly similar.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our findings highlight the impact of fundamental quantum physics constraints on the long-term performance of quantum computing applications in data science. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The scalability of QRAM is questioned when considering the relativistic constraints on qubits interacting locally. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: The publication reframes the broad conclusion about performance impacts into a more specific limitation on QRAM scalability under relativistic constraints, reducing scope and certainty compared with the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We suggest potential quantum memory designs for performance enhancement. (certainty=low, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: There is a need for innovative quantum memory solutions to navigate foundational barriers in quantum computing. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication generalizes the preprint's suggestion into a stronger, higher-certainty call for innovative memory solutions; the intent is the same but the published wording is broader and stated with greater emphasis.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Attention models learned using soft attention loss improve quickly at initialization but splutter later on. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Attention models learned using soft attention loss improve quickly at initialization but splutter later on. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reproduces this experimental observation verbatim with the same certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Hard attention loss behaves in the opposite fashion to soft attention loss. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Hard attention loss behaves in the opposite fashion to soft attention loss. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this comparative behavior claim identically.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The distinct dynamics and final results of the three attention paradigms are due to the way selected segments are aggregated. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A unique signature of models learned using soft attention, hard attention, and LVML attention is observed and explained as a consequence of the evolution of the classification model under gradient descent. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication offers a different mechanistic explanation (evolution under gradient descent) for the observed signatures rather than attributing them to segment-aggregation, so the original explanatory claim is revised.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A simple hybrid approach that combines the advantages of different loss functions is proposed and demonstrated on various datasets. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A simple hybrid approach that combines the advantages of different loss functions is proposed and demonstrated on semi-synthetic and real-world datasets. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication clarifies the kinds of datasets used (semi-synthetic and real-world) while retaining the same contribution and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed ASCON framework explores anatomical semantics for low-dose CT denoising while providing anatomical interpretability. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed ASCON framework explores anatomical semantics for low-dose CT denoising while providing anatomical interpretability. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same contribution claim verbatim, so there is no semantic, scope, or confidence change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: ASCON consists of an efficient self-attention-based U-Net (ESAU-Net) and a multi-scale anatomical contrastive network (MAC-Net). (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: ASCON consists of an efficient self-attention-based U-Net (ESAU-Net) and a multi-scale anatomical contrastive network (MAC-Net). (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The architecture description is identical in preprint and publication.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Extensive experimental results demonstrate superior performance of ASCON over state-of-the-art models for low-dose CT denoising. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The result claim is carried over unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: ASCON provides anatomical interpretability for low-dose CT denoising for the first time. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: ASCON provides anatomical interpretability for low-dose CT denoising for the first time. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same concluding claim without change.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Finding whether a winning strategy exists for timeline-based games is 2EXPTIME-complete. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.rationale: The contribution claim is the same in both preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The introduction of the offset block in the model results in significantly improved performance compared to the vanilla kernel prediction network (KPN). (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The introduction of the offset block results in significantly improved performance compared to the vanilla kernel prediction network. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The method shows only a small average error of 0.27-0.54° C relative to costly scientific-grade radiometric cameras. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The numerical real-data error range is the same in both documents, so this result is unchanged.

alignments.pub_claim_text: CommonsenseVIS is a visual explanatory system that utilizes external commonsense knowledge bases to contextualize model behavior for commonsense question-answering. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.pub_claim_text: CommonsenseVIS helps NLP experts conduct a systematic and scalable visual analysis of models' relational reasoning over concepts in different situations. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Feature attributions cannot infer models' implicit reasoning over mentioned concepts due to the implicit nature of commonsense knowledge. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Feature attributions cannot infer models' implicit reasoning over mentioned concepts due to the implicit nature of commonsense knowledge. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed Uncertainty-Aware Network (UANet) alleviates the problem of uncertain predictions in building extraction. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The new method, Stochastic Dimension Gradient Descent (SDGD), can solve many notoriously hard high-dimensional PDEs very fast on a single GPU using the PINNs mesh-free approach.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method demonstrates the ability to solve high-dimensional PDEs, including the Hamilton-Jacobi-Bellman and Schrödinger equations, very quickly on a single GPU.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the claim that SDGD solves high-dimensional PDEs quickly on a single GPU and adds specific example PDEs, clarifying the original performance claim without changing its scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We prove theoretically the convergence and other desired properties of the proposed method.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The theoretical convergence and other desired properties of the proposed method are proven.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: SDGD allows for the solution of nonlinear PDEs with nontrivial, anisotropic, and inseparable solutions in 100,000 effective dimensions in 12 hours on a single GPU.
alignments.pub_claim_text: SDGD allows for the solution of nonlinear PDEs with complex solutions in significantly reduced time, achieving solutions in less than one hour for 1000 dimensions and in 12 hours for 100,000 dimensions.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the 100,000-dimension / 12-hour result from the preprint and adds an additional performance datapoint (1000 dims <1 hour), clarifying performance without changing the original claim's scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: SDGD is a general training methodology of PINNs that can be applied to any current and future variants of PINNs to scale them up for arbitrary high-dimensional PDEs.
alignments.pub_claim_text: SDGD is a general training methodology that can be applied to any current and future variants of PINNs for scaling up to high-dimensional PDEs.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the generality claim about SDGD applying across PINN variants to scale to high-dimensional PDEs, essentially unchanged in meaning.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Constraining the physical model of the camera with the neural network lowered the error by an additional 0.1°C.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this specific error reduction claim unchanged in wording and magnitude.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The mean temperature error over an extensive validation dataset was 0.37°C.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The method uses an action recognition network to estimate athletes’ cumulative race time during an ultra-distance competition.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Global Density Feature Refinement (GDFR) module refines features by pushing features with different global densities further away. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The haze density difference can guide networks to perceive different global densities and focus on local areas with high density or that are difficult to dehaze. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The publication frames the GDFR behavior as guiding networks to perceive different global densities and focus on challenging local areas, which clarifies the module-level description from the preprint.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Intermediate Dehazing Residual Feedforward (IDRF) module updates local features and pulls them closer to clear image features. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Density Feature Refinement Network (DFR-Net) extracts haze density features from density differences and leverages these differences to refine density features. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication describes the network-level use of density differences to refine features; this corresponds to the IDRF's local refinement behavior but is expressed at a slightly more general level.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method for translating social scientific constructs into AI objective functions, termed societal objective functions, can effectively be applied to the political science construct of anti-democratic attitudes. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The method for translating social scientific constructs into AI objective functions can effectively create a democratic attitude model. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication narrows the generic formulation language to the concrete outcome (creating a democratic attitude model), clarifying the preprint's methodological claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Removal and downranking of social media feeds based on anti-democratic attitude scores reduced participants' partisan animosity without compromising their experience and engagement. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Downranking social media feeds based on anti-democratic attitude scores reduces partisan animosity without compromising user experience. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the same experimental outcome (reduced partisan animosity without compromising user experience), so the result is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The democratic attitude model created in Study 2 showed strong agreement with manual labels, indicating its reliability. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The democratic attitude model shows strong agreement with manual labels for anti-democratic attitudes. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication affirms the model's strong agreement with manual labels, matching the preprint's claim of reliability.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Using the democratic attitude model in Study 3 again demonstrated that feed downranking reduced partisan animosity. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Downranking social media feeds based on anti-democratic attitude scores reduces partisan animosity without compromising user experience. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication reports a repeated experimental finding that downranking reduces partisan animosity; this aligns directly with the preprint's Study 3 claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: This paper studies a model of infinite-horizon MDPs with time-varying discount factors.
alignments.pub_claim_text: This paper presents a constructive proof of the existence of a subgame perfect equilibrium (SPE) in infinite-horizon MDPs with time-varying discount factors.
alignments.rationale: The publication situates the constructive proof specifically in the infinite-horizon MDPs with time-varying discounts described by the preprint, clarifying the domain of study.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We present a constructive proof of the existence of a subgame perfect equilibrium (SPE).
alignments.pub_claim_text: This paper presents a constructive proof of the existence of a subgame perfect equilibrium (SPE) in infinite-horizon MDPs with time-varying discount factors.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the preprint's constructive existence proof while explicitly naming the MDP setting, so the core claim is preserved but slightly clarified.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We demonstrate the EXPTIME-hardness of computing an SPE.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The computation of a subgame perfect equilibrium (SPE) is EXPTIME-hard.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same complexity hardness result without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: An epsilon-SPE exists under milder assumptions.
alignments.pub_claim_text: An epsilon-subgame perfect equilibrium (epsilon-SPE) exists under milder assumptions than those required for an SPE.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the existence of an epsilon-SPE under milder assumptions and makes explicit the comparison to SPE assumptions, preserving the original claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: An algorithm is presented to compute an epsilon-SPE, with an upper bound of the time complexity provided.
alignments.pub_claim_text: An algorithm is provided to compute an epsilon-SPE, with an upper bound on the time complexity based on the convergence property of the time-varying discount factor.
alignments.rationale: The publication gives the same algorithmic claim but clarifies the source of the complexity bound (convergence property), refining the original statement.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed Preference Score (PS) serves as a quantitative evaluation metric that aligns with human preference without the need for human annotation.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed Preference Score (PS) serves as a quantitative evaluation metric that aligns with human preference without the need for human annotation.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same contribution claim verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Experimental results validate the superiority of Preference Score in aligning with human perception.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Experimental results validate the superiority of Preference Score in aligning with human perception.
alignments.rationale: The experimental validation claim is preserved exactly in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Preference Score demonstrates robustness and generalizability to unseen data.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Preference Score demonstrates robustness and generalizability to unseen data.
alignments.rationale: The robustness and generalizability claim is unchanged between versions.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The limitation of existing quantitative evaluations hinders the advancement of conversational head generation algorithms and systems.
alignments.rationale: The limitation noted in the preprint is carried into the publication without change.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Knowledge graphs are effective for modeling entities and their relationships through the use of ontologies.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the same concluding claim about knowledge graphs and ontologies.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The incorporation of users and items into knowledge graphs allows recommender systems to better capture implicit connections and provide more accurate recommendations.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method of constructing a personalized recommender system via knowledge graphs embedding is effective in providing relevant recommendations consistent with individual users.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed methods achieved new state-of-the-art classification accuracies on three web image datasets. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: When gradients are not bounded, the convergence rate of DASGD is $\text{O}(\tau\text{\epsilon}^{-2}) + \text{O}(\text{sqrt}(\hat{S}_{avg}\hat{S}_{max})\text{\epsilon}^{-1})$. (certainty=high, type=result)
